concerns.text: T021 implements a 'Geometry-Based Baseline' using RDKit's 3D conformer generation. This contradicts the plan.md (Summary & Complexity Tracking) which explicitly defines the baseline as a 'Random Forest on 2D descriptors' to avoid circularity. T021 consumes 3D geometry as input, violating the '2D-only' hypothesis test defined in the spec/plan. The task must be rewritten to implement the Random Forest baseline on 2D features, not a 3D calculation.

T028 implements a sensitivity analysis sweeping 'relative' MAE thresholds ({1%, 5%, 10%}). This violates FR-006 in spec.md which mandates an 'absolute' threshold sweep ({0.01, 0.05, 0.1} Å²). While the plan.md notes this conflict, the tasks document must either implement the spec requirement or include a specific task to formally override the spec before execution. Currently, T028 consumes a non-existent 'relative' metric definition without resolving the spec conflict.

Tasks T010, T018, and T026 are marked [P] (parallel-safe) and depend on 'static schema.yaml', 'model_schema.yaml', and 'sensitivity_schema.yaml' respectively. These schema files are not defined in spec.md, plan.md, or tasks.md. A consumer task (the test) cannot run in parallel or at all without the producer task (schema definition) preceding it. These tasks are currently impossible to execute as written.

T015 implements data splitting with a KS test condition (p-value > 0.05). However, there is no preceding task that generates the 'Molecular Weight' feature column required to perform the stratification and the KS test. T013 extracts 2D graph features, but T015 assumes the MW column exists in the processed dataset. A task to explicitly calculate and persist Molecular Weight from the SMILES/graph data must precede T015.

T030 generates the sensitivity report and depends on T027a (scale analysis). However, T027a is listed as a sub-task of T027 (Unit test for Bonferroni) in the text, but T027 is a test task. T027a is not a distinct, executable implementation task in the list. T030 consumes an artifact (scale analysis) that has no dedicated producer task in the implementation flow.
